rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Parex USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Familian Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Navarro, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Atkinson¿s declaration are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to its own General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Flowserve Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s written contention discovery (which the court notes did not include responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not meet its burden of production under the affirmative evidence prong of Aguilar because Mr. Adams¿ declaration does not affirmatively negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production to make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Ms. Ringo¿s testimony are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Metcalf¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Elliott Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Fisher Controls International LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues (negligence, strict liability, false representation and loss of consortium). As to negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium, Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. As to false representation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant made any false representation. Defendant¿s separate statement regarding adjudication of false representation failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery regarding their false representation cause of action, so defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Aurora Pump Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Metcalf¿s declaration are overruled. Further, defendant did not raise substantial factor as an issue in its separate statement. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 (strict liability and negligence) and off calendar as to issues 5 (false representation) and 6 (punitive damages). As to strict liability and negligence, Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs have represented that they are dismissing their false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
